Or is the code fine and the\n   comment based on a misread?\n2. **Is the severity calibrated?** — Is the reviewer overstating the impact?\n   A `Critical` on a missing null check in an internal utility that can never\n   receive null is not critical.\n3. **Is the comment in-scope?** — Comments on formatting, naming, hypothetical\n   future requirements, or pure style preferences should be challenged.\n4. **Is the proposed fix actually better?** — If the reviewer proposes a\n   specific change, is it demonstrably better or just different?\n\nDo **not** push back on comments that identify clear bugs, genuine security\nissues, or real performance problems with solid reasoning. Affirm those silently\n— don't post agreement for its own sake.\n\n## How to gather context\n\n- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — PR metadata.\n- `gh api repos/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments` — list all inline review\n  comments. Note each comment's `id` field — you'll\n  need it to post replies.\n- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff, for verifying whether reviewer claims\n  hold up against the actual code.\n- Read files in the working directory as needed for context around flagged lines.\n\n### Verifying external facts\n\nDo not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —\nlibrary APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.\n\n- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7\n  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).\n  Prefer this over web search for docs.\n- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on\n  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages.\n\nIf you can't verify a claim, don't use it to push back.\n\n## How to respond\n\n### Inline reply (primary)\n\nReply to specific comments where you have a concrete counter-argument:\n\n```\ngh api repos/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments/${COMMENT_ID}/replies \\\n  --method POST \\\n  --field body=\"...\"\n```\n\nOne reply per comment you are pushing back on. Each reply must:\n\n- State concisely **why** the comment is wrong, over-eager, or low-signal.\n- Reference the actual code or diff to support your counter-claim.\n- If the reviewer proposes a fix, explain what's wrong with it and (if you have\n  a better alternative) show what would be preferable and why.\n\nKeep replies factual and direct. No hedging, no apologies, no padding. Escape\nany backticks in the `--field body` value as `\\`` to avoid shell interpretation.\n\n### Top-level summary (optional)\n\nIf there's a recurring pattern to the reviewer's over-reach (e.g. consistently\nflagging idiomatic style as non-idiomatic, or raising hypothetical edge cases\nthat the codebase explicitly doesn't support), post one short top-level comment\nvia `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body \"...\"` summarizing the pattern. Keep it\nbrief — a few sentences. Skip this entirely if there's no pattern worth naming.\nEscape any backticks in `--body` as `\\`` to avoid shell interpretation.\n\nIf all inline comments are valid, post nothing and stop.\n\n## When you're done\n\nStop immediately after your last tool call. Do not summarize what you did,\ndo not list which comments you replied to, do not confirm the reply count.\nThe runner will exit.\n"}

   plausible to run.
5. Commit: `git add <paths>` then `git commit -m "<message>"`. Small
   focused commits or a single squash-style commit are both fine.
   Don't commit broken code.
6. Push and open PR (below).

## Finishing

```sh
git push -u origin HEAD
gh pr create --draft \
  --base main \
  --title "[${TICKET_KEY}] <one-line summary>" \
  --body "<body>"
```

**PR body** (GitHub-flavored markdown) — include:

- **Acceptance criteria** — tick the AC items the change addresses

  from `.jira/config.yml` with the `Read` tool — don't `cat` via

- Touching `.github/agent-logs/`.

  blocker (see below) — do not retry with workarounds.

Stop trying to push the change. Open a `[WIP]` Draft PR documenting
the blocker:

1. If `git push` worked but `gh pr create` failed: the branch is on

        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          # PAT with workflows:write so the agent can push branches that
          # add or modify .github/workflows/*. Falls back to GITHUB_TOKEN
          # when the PAT isn't configured (workflow-file changes will then
          # be rejected at push time, by design).
          token: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install jira CLI
        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh

      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Write jira config
        env:
          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
          mkdir -p .jira
          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
          installation: Cloud
          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
          project:
              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
              type: classic
          board:
              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
          EOF

      - name: Install Claude Code CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

      - name: Configure git user
        run: |
          git config --global user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config --global user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Create feature branch
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -B "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main
          # Reset the remote ref so re-runs of the same ticket start
          # clean — matches the local `checkout -B` semantic. A prior
          # failed run can leave commits on the remote that diverge
          # from current main, which would block fast-forward push.
          git push --force origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}"

      - id: prompt
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}"
            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
            echo
            cat agents/junior-dev/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        name: Run Claude (headless)
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          CONTEXT7_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PROMPT: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          MAX_TURNS: ${{ inputs.max_turns }}
        run: |
          set +e
          claude -p "$PROMPT" \
            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \
            --output-format stream-json \
            --verbose \
            --allowedTools "Bash(jira issue view:*),Bash(bin/jira issue view:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git status:*),Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(git rev-parse:*),Bash(git remote:*),Bash(git config --get:*),Bash(git config --list),Bash(git branch:*),Bash(git show:*),Bash(gh pr create:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh auth status),Bash(gh api repos/:*),Bash(npm test:*),Bash(npm run:*),Bash(ls:*),Bash(cat:*),Bash(printenv:*),Bash(env),Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch" \
            > stream.jsonl
          rc=$?
          echo "claude_exit=$rc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # Always succeed; downstream steps handle non-zero claude exit.
          exit 0

      - name: Convert log to JSON array
        if: always()
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ -s stream.jsonl ]; then
            jq -s '.' stream.jsonl > execution.json
          else
            echo '[]' > execution.json
          fi

      - name: Open fallback Draft PR if agent didn't
        if: always()
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Skip if the agent already opened a PR for this branch.
          if gh pr view "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" --json url >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "PR already exists for junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping fallback."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Need at least one commit beyond origin/main to have something to push.
          commits=$(git rev-list --count "origin/main..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
          if [ "$commits" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "No commits on branch; nothing to push."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Push the branch (no-op if the agent already pushed).
          git push -u origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" || true

          gh pr create --draft \
            --base main \
            --head "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
            --title "[WIP] [${TICKET_KEY}] junior-dev exited before opening PR" \
            --body "Junior-dev agent run terminated before opening a PR itself (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). Branch holds whatever the agent committed. See \`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/\` for the run log."

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp execution.json "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: junior-dev run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=junior-dev \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref=main

You implement Jira tickets in this repository. The ticket key is
prepended above as `TICKET_KEY`. A feature branch is already checked
out at `origin/main`. Your job: implement the change, commit, push,
and open a Draft PR. Then stop.

## Environment

- `jira`, `gh`, `git`, `claude` on `PATH`. `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and
  `JIRA_API_TOKEN` set; `GH_TOKEN` set; git user configured.
- `jira issue view` is read-only — never call mutating jira commands.
- CWD is the repo on the dispatched feature branch. Don't switch
  branches; don't force-push.
- The Bash allowlist only permits the specific patterns listed in the
  workflow. Compound commands (pipes, `&&`, `;`-chained, sub-shells)
  are not allowed and will be rejected by the harness — run each step
  as its own Bash call. Don't try to bypass this.

## Workflow

1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain`. Note
   summary, description, acceptance criteria.
2. Plan: open the files/symbols the ticket references with `Read`,
   `Glob`, `Grep`. Verify paths exist before assuming.
3. Implement: edit with `Edit`/`Write`. Stay scoped to what the
   ticket asks for. No drive-by refactoring.
4. Verify: if the change has obvious tests or a lint command, run
   them (`npm test`, `npm run lint`). Skip if there's nothing
   plausible to run.
5. Commit: `git add <paths>` then `git commit -m "<message>"`. Small
   focused commits or a single squash-style commit are both fine.
   Don't commit broken code.
6. Push and open PR (below).

## Finishing

```sh
git push -u origin HEAD
gh pr create --draft \
  --base main \
  --title "[${TICKET_KEY}] <one-line summary>" \
  --body "<body>"
```

If you're stopping early because of the turn cap or a blocker, prefix
the title with `[WIP]` and explain in the body.

**PR body** (GitHub-flavored markdown) — include:

- **Summary** — 1–3 bullets on what changed and why.

gh pr create --base main --title "Prompt fix: junior-dev — scope exploration + prefer incremental commits" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
In run `.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/2026-06-15/27537999970.json` (max_turns=5 stop-condition test), the agent used all 5 turns on exploration and writing one file — hitting `error_max_turns` with nothing committed, so the fallback step had no commits to push. Two concrete causes: (1) the agent read `docs/agentic-workflow.md` speculatively (the ticket didn't reference it), consuming a turn that could have gone to committing; (2) the agent batched all files into a single end-of-run commit rather than committing after each file, so partial work was lost when the turn cap hit.

This PR adds two sentences to the prompt: one telling the agent to limit exploration to files the ticket explicitly references, and one changing the commit guidance from "either incremental or squash is fine" to "prefer committing after each meaningful chunk so partial progress survives a turn-cap hit."
EOF
)"

content.text:
PR open: https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/55

Two prompt changes in `agents/junior-dev/prompt.md`:
1. **Step 2 (Plan)**: Added "only read files the ticket explicitly references — do not speculatively open docs/, architecture files, or unrelated directories." This directly addresses the agent burning a turn on `docs/agentic-workflow.md` to "check model IDs" that weren't there.
2. **Step 5 (Commit)**: Changed "small focused commits or a single squash-style commit are both fine" to "commit after each meaningful chunk rather than waiting until all files are done — this preserves partial progress if the run hits the turn limit." With the old guidance the agent deferred all commits to the end and lost all work when the turn cap hit; with the new guidance, even a short-turn run captures something the fallback step can push.
